name: Farsha Capital Amer Bus Group
owner_or_parent: Farsha Capital American Business Group Inc (NY corp; service of process c/o Fauzia Shaheen)
recent_news: No news, contracts, or public bus-operator records found tying this NY corporation to active school-bus service; entity was incorporated 2017 (not 1970 as in source data) and registered an assumed name 'Wash Lab' in 2017; NY DOS biennial statement marked Past Due.
fit_notes: Weak target. No evidence this is an active, contracted NYC school-bus operator; appears to be a shell/holding entity at a Bronx address with unclear bus operations. Bronx-area NYC DOE contractors are typically union (Local 1181), with limited tuck-in appeal at this scale. Recommend deprioritize pending fleet/contract confirmation.

name: King's Transit
districts_or_customers: Greene County / Waynesburg, PA area (likely Central Greene SD, Carmichaels Area SD)
recent_news: No M&A or news reported; BBB lists 27 years in business, not BBB accredited, insufficient information for rating; 4.8-star aggregate review profile.
fit_notes: Small SW PA family operator near WV border, sub-scale (~22 employees, est. $0.8M revenue). Founder/President Harold King Jr suggests potential successor risk. Geography (Waynesburg) is outside dense Summit footprint but could be part of a PA roll-up; likely too small as a standalone tuck-in absent cluster strategy.

name: Safeway Transportation
owner_or_parent: independent (founded by group of former taxicab owners)
districts_or_customers: Detroit Public Schools Community District (DPSCD) - long-term contract, ~35+ years; dedicated 80 vehicles to DPSCD routes through 2020 and beyond
recent_news: Featured in School Transportation News for investing in 50 Blue Bird propane school buses, targeting 100% green fleet; long-tenured DPSCD contract; 2003 Michigan COA case (Safeway v. Essie) involving non-compete dispute with former officials.
fit_notes: Strong tuck-in candidate: 50-year-old independent Detroit operator with deep DPSCD special-needs contract, ~$11M revenue, propane-fleet investment story, and long-tenured female President (Patricia Whitlow) suggesting elevated succession likelihood. Single-district concentration risk and prior NLRB/labor history are diligence items. Excellent SPED platform addition for Summit in Michigan.

name: Cozy Van
principals: James M. Diesburg (primary contact, FMCSA filings)
districts_or_customers: Northeast Iowa region (Cedar Falls, Waterloo, New Hampton, tri-state); Medicaid NEMT; not a primary school-bus contractor
recent_news: Original cozyvan.com domain is no longer active (redirects to a domain marketplace), suggesting possible wind-down or web-presence lapse; USDOT 1851766 active with 11 power units / 11 drivers, intrastate passenger authority only.
fit_notes: Weak fit for Summit. This is a NEMT / wheelchair-van operator (Medicaid transport), not a yellow-bus school contractor; ~11 vehicles, ~$1.2M revenue. Founder-aged owner (Maryann Diesburg, business dates back to 1970) and dormant website signal succession opportunity, but service mix and rural Iowa geography are off-thesis for Summit's school-bus tuck-in strategy.

name: Comtrans
owner_or_parent: American Medical Response (AMR) / Global Medical Response (GMR) - acquired ComTrans Sept 16, 2016; GMR is PE-backed (KKR)
principals: Edward Van Horne (President, per source data)
districts_or_customers: Arizona behavioral health organizations and DCS; school-age behavioral health students; operations also in Oregon (Milwaukie) and Texas (Arlington)
recent_news: Between 2020-2024 ComTrans/AMR downsized most Arizona contracts due to financial deficits; only Crisis service line continues in AZ; other AZ business lines shut down. AMR sits under Global Medical Response (KKR-backed) post-2018 AMGH merger.
fit_notes: Not a tuck-in target as currently structured: owned by AMR/Global Medical Response (KKR-backed strategic). However, the 2020-2024 AZ downsizing and shuttering of non-crisis lines may indicate a carve-out / divestiture opportunity if Summit wants to enter AZ SPED/behavioral-health transport. Worth a relationship call with GMR corp dev rather than direct owner outreach.
